bird.name: Hooded vulture
bird.summary: The hooded vulture (Necrosyrtes monachus) is an Old World vulture in the order Accipitriformes, which also includes eagles, kites, buzzards…

locations.name: 撒哈拉以南非洲
locations.summary: 撒哈拉以南非洲（英語：Sub-Saharan Africa）指撒哈拉沙漠以南的非洲地區，也稱黑非洲（Negro Africa）。自然地理上與衣索比亞界高度重疊。政治上，包括了所有或部分领土位于撒哈拉沙漠以南的国家（苏丹除外，尽管位于撒哈拉…

locations.name: 南部非洲
locations.summary: 南部非洲（英语：Southern Africa）即非洲大陆南部地区，不常简称為南非，“南非”（英语：South Africa）通常指南非共和国。联合国地理方案的南部非洲次分区包括下列五個南部非洲关税同盟国家： 博茨瓦纳 史瓦帝尼（斯威士兰…

locations.name: 东非
locations.summary: 东非即非洲的东部地区，根據聯合國的次分區共有18個国家或屬地： 埃塞俄比亞、索馬里（含索馬利蘭）、厄立特里亚和吉布提，以上几國在地理上稱為非洲之角 坦桑尼亚、肯尼亚、乌干达、卢旺达、布隆迪和南苏丹，以上六國組成東非共同體 赞比亚、津巴布韦…

locations.name: 西非
locations.summary: 西非通常是指非洲大陆南北分界线和向西凸起部分的大片地区，为地理、人种和文化过渡地带。 非洲大陆南大西洋海岸线在这一地区呈东西走向的部分曾以象牙海岸、黄金海岸而闻名。奴隶贸易在这里被人操纵，现在绝大多数非洲裔美国人就是当时从西非贩运到美国的…

locations.name: 纳米比亚
locations.summary: 納米比亞共和國（德語：Republik Namibia，英語：Republic of Namibia），通稱納米比亞（Namibia），是位於南部非洲西面的共和國。它西部瀕臨大西洋，北部接壤贊比亞和安哥拉，東部連接博茨瓦纳，而在南部則與南…

locations.name: 波札那
locations.summary: 博茨瓦纳共和国（英語：Republic of Botswana；茨瓦納語：Lefatshe la Botswana），通稱博茨瓦纳（Botswana），是位於非洲南部的內陸國家，全國國境皆為乾燥的台地地形，卡拉哈里沙漠占据了该国大約70%…

locations.name: 赞比亚
locations.summary: 赞比亚共和国（英語：Republic of Zambia），也稱尚比亞（英語：Zambia），是非洲東南部的一個內陸國家。与刚果民主共和国、坦桑尼亚、马拉维、莫桑比克、津巴布韦、博茨瓦纳、纳米比亚和安哥拉相邻。首都是卢萨卡。 历史 二千年…

locations.name: 辛巴威
locations.summary: 津巴布韦共和國（英語：Republic of Zimbabwe），通稱津巴布韋（Zimbabwe），是位於非洲南部的內陸國家，地处赞比西河与林波波河之间，南接南非，西部及西南部与博茨瓦纳接壤，西北与赞比亚接壤，东部及东北部与莫桑比克相邻，…

locations.name: 莫桑比克
locations.summary: 莫桑比克共和国（葡萄牙語：República de Moçambique，發音：[ʁɛˈpuβlikɐ ðɨ musɐ̃ˈbikɨ]），舊譯作莫三鼻給，現通稱莫三比克或莫桑比克，位於非洲東部，临印度洋，隔莫桑比克海峡与马达加斯加相望，以葡…

locations.name: 南非
locations.summary: 南非共和國（阿非利卡語：Republiek van Suid-Afrika [rɛpɵˈblik fan səitˈafrikɑː]；英語：Republic of South Africa），通稱南非（阿非利卡語：Suid-Afrika…

locations.name: 克鲁格国家公园
locations.summary: 克魯格國家公園（英語：Kruger National Park）是南非的國家公園，佔地19,485平方公里（7,523平方英里），是非洲最大的自然保護區之一。它是以南非共和國總統保罗·克鲁格名字命名。 历史 成立於1926年5月31日，成…

locations.name: 姆普马兰加省
locations.summary: 姆普馬蘭加省（阿非利卡語：Mpumalanga，英語：Mpumalanga），是南非九個省之一，前身是德蘭士瓦省的東部。該省東部毗鄰莫桑比克與史瓦濟蘭。面積74,490平方公里，2001年人口為3,122,994人。首府及最大城市姆邦貝拉…

locations.name: 林波波省
locations.summary: 林波波省（阿非利卡語：Limpopo，英語：Limpopo），是南非九個省之一，前身是德蘭士瓦省的北部。面積125,754平方公里，2022年人口為6,572,720人。首府及最大城市为波洛夸内，而省立法机构则位于莱博瓦霍莫。 历史 地理…

locations.name: 夸祖魯-納塔爾省
locations.summary: 夸祖魯-納塔爾省（阿非利卡語：KwaZulu-Natal，英語：KwaZulu-Natal，通常簡稱為KZN），是南非九個省之一，在1994年由納塔爾省與夸祖魯合併而成。該省西部毗鄰賴索托，北部接壤莫桑比克以及斯威士蘭，而東部則與印度洋相…

locations.name: 卡格拉格帝跨境公园
locations.summary: 卡格拉格帝边境公园是南非大型野生生物保护区。公园跨坐于南非和博茨瓦纳的边界，并包含了两座相连的国家公园： 南非喀拉哈里大羚羊国家公园 博茨瓦纳大羚羊国家公园 公园总面积为38,000km²。接近3/4的部分位于博茨瓦纳境内，1/4的面积则…

relations.subject: Hooded vulture
relations.evidence: | date=2024 | title=Enigmas no longer: using ultraconserved elements to place several unusual hawk taxa and address the non-monophyly of the genus Accipiter (Accipitriformes: Accipitridae) | journal=Biological Journal of the Linnean Society | article-number=blae028 | doi=10.1093/biolinnean/blae028}} It is native to sub-Saharan Africa, where it has a widespread distribution with populations in southern, East and West Africa.{{Cite journal |last1=Reading |first1=Richard P

relations.subject: Hooded vulture
relations.evidence: It occurs in Senegal, Mauritania, Guinea-Bissau, The Gambia, Niger and Nigeria in West Africa; in East Africa it is found in Chad, Sudan, South Sudan, Ethiopia, Rwanda and Somalia; in southern Africa it has been recorded in northern Namibia, Botswana, Zambia, Zimbabwe, Mozambique and South Africa. Within South Africa, the species is essentially restricted to the Kruger National Park and surrounding protected areas in Mpumalanga and Limpopo provinces, though vagrants have been recorded further west in Kwa-Zulu Natal and Kgalagadi Transfrontier Park

relations.subject: Hooded vulture
relations.evidence: status = CR

relations.subject: Hooded vulture
relations.evidence: |title=The decline of an urban Hooded Vulture Necrosyrtes monachus population in Dakar, Senegal, over 50 years |journal=Ostrich |volume=88 |issue=2 |year=2017 |pages=131–138|doi=10.2989/00306525.2017.1333538 |bibcode=2017Ostri..88..131M |s2cid=90555471 }} Threats include poisoning, hunting, loss of habitat and collisions with electricity infrastructure, and the International Union for Conservation of Nature has rated its conservation status as "critically endangered" in their latest assessment (2022). The highest current regional density of hooded vultures is in the western region of The Gambia
relations.object_summary: 直接猎捕或误捕造成额外死亡。

relations.subject: Hooded vulture
relations.evidence: The species has been uplisted from its previous IUCN status of endangered to critically endangered, since the species is going through a very steep decline in population, owing to various factors including poisoning, hunting, habitat loss and degradation of habitat. Hunting is the most well-known threat to the species, however, poisoning has been shown to have the highest impact on the population
relations.object_summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。

relations.subject: Hooded vulture
relations.evidence: They found that 40% of traders were selling the vultures for spiritual healing and 25% for human consumption. Many West and southern African cultures believe vulture body parts cure a range of physical and mental illnesses, improve success in gambling and business ventures, or increase intelligence in children. Consumption of vultures as bushmeat in Nigeria and Ivory Coast may be of regional concern, but smoked vulture meat is traded and consumed internationally. Secondary poisoning with carbofuran pesticides at livestock baits being used to poison mammalian predators is also an issue in East Africa. On the 20th of June 2019, the carcasses of 468 white-backed vultures, 17 white-headed vultures, 28 hooded vultures, 14 lappet-faced vultures and 10 cape vultures), altogether 537 vultures, besides 2 tawny eagles, were found in northern Botswana
relations.object_summary: 农业化学品通过水体和食物链影响鸟类生存。

relations.subject: Hooded vulture
relations.evidence: It is suspected that they died after eating the carcasses of 3 elephants that were poisoned by poachers, possibly to avoid detection by the birds, which help rangers to track poaching activity by circling over the locations of dead animals. The species may also be threatened by avian influenza (H5N1), from which it appears to suffer some mortality and which it probably acquires from feeding on discarded dead poultry.{{Cite journal|last1=Ducatez|first1=Mariette F
relations.object_summary: 直接捕杀或非法贸易导致种群下降。
